action.reasoning: Observation: the self-distillation stage substantially hurt test accuracy (0.7881 -> 0.7559) while also taking ~11.7 minutes, and noisy-val accuracy was lower than baseline. Analysis: with heavily noisy labels, naive pseudo-label mixing likely reinforced model mistakes; the stronger signal is that the simple pretrained ConvNeXt baseline already generalizes well. A safer next move is to improve robustness with sample selection rather than relabeling: train on all data for warmup, then continue using generalized cross-entropy plus confidence-based small-loss filtering, while also using test-time augmentation at inference. This is incremental, lower risk than pseudo-label replacement, and should fit budget. Decision: simplify train.py to a warmup+small-loss robust training recipe, add TTA to predict.py, train a moderate run starting from pretrained weights, smoke test locally on val.pt, and submit the new checkpoint.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 8 --warmup_epochs 3 --batch_size 96 --lr 3e-4 --keep_ratio 0.7 --gce_q 0.7 --save_path checkpoints/smallloss.pt && mkdir -p local_eval && cp /data/val.pt local_eval/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path local_eval --checkpoint checkpoints/smallloss.pt --output smoke_preds.json
